sub_strands.sub_strand: Talking About Technology, Safety, and Communication
outcomes: Express complex ideas, instructions, and safety precautions clearly using a wide range of vocabulary
Listen critically to short informational texts to summarize key facts and express opinions
Use pronouns, prepositions, and simple past tense regular/irregular verbs accurately in speech
Respond appropriately to open-ended questions regarding familiar topics
Read grade-level texts at an optimal speed with appropriate phrasing, tone, and accuracy
Demonstrate sustained silent reading for pleasure and information retrieval
Make inferences, predict outcomes, and distinguish between fact and fiction in reading cards and storybooks
Identify the central theme, moral lesson, and sequence of events from a text
Write cohesive short paragraphs using correct sentence structures, capitalization, and punctuation
Spell unfamiliar multi-syllabic words accurately using phonetic spelling strategies

learning_areas.learning_area: Kiswahili Activities (Shughuli za Lugha ya Kiswahili)

outcomes: Kusikiliza hadithi, habari, au maagizo na kutoa muhtasari au majibu sahihi ya maswali
Kutumia msamiati mwafaka kueleza matukio, usalama, na mazingira yanayomzunguka mwanafunzi
Kusoma vifungu vya maneno kwa sauti inayosikika na kwa kasi inayofaa huku wakizingatia uakifishaji
Kujibu maswali ya ufahamu kutokana na vifungu vya kusoma (maswali ya moja kwa moja na ya mapendeleo)
Kuandika aya fupi na tungo rahisi zenye mtiririko mzuri wa mawazo na tahajia sahihi
Count forward and backward from 1 to 1000 in intervals of 2, 5, 10, 50, and 100
Read, write, and represent numbers up to 1000 in symbols and numbers up to 100 in words
Identify place value of digits up to thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones
Identify, read, and write simple fractions as part of a whole (half, quarter, eighth)
Add up to three-digit numbers with regrouping up to a sum of 1000
Subtract up to three-digit numbers with regrouping within 1000
Multiply single-digit numbers by numbers up to 10 using mental strategies and arrays
Divide numbers up to 30 by single digits without a remainder through factor grouping
Measure length in standard units (meters) using tape measures or meter rules
Measure mass in kilograms using a simple weighing balance
Measure and compare capacity in liters using standard containers
Tell time to the nearest five minutes using an analogue clock face
Perform operations involving Kenyan currency notes and coins up to 100 shillings
Identify and trace horizontal, vertical, and diagonal lines
Make patterns using interlocking two-dimensional shapes
Model and compare physical properties of clay, sand, and loam soils based on texture and modeling capability
Demonstrate simple methods of conserving soil erosion in the school environment
Identify local sources of clean and polluted water and discuss methods of safe harvesting
Practice practical water conservation measures like turning off taps and recycling water
Describe common business transactions, types of items sold, and basic ethics in the local market
Demonstrate resource planning by reusing and recycling materials within their environment
Identify common external human parasites (lice, bedbugs, jiggers) and state regular preventive actions
Demonstrate appropriate methods of medicine usage, storage, and adherence to safety labels
Identify potential hazards in a kitchen and demonstrate actions to prevent burns or accidental cuts
Practice traditional and modern simple methods of preserving common food items from contamination
Explain the importance of respecting institutional authority, community elders, and parents based on spiritual teachings
Demonstrate honesty, inclusivity, and empathy when dealing with individuals of different backgrounds
Execute proper body buoyancy techniques, kicking patterns, and water safety strokes independently
Perform sequences of specialized physical maneuvers including forward rolls, vaulting, and balancing with control
Design and construct basic hand puppets, masks, or beaded ornaments using local sustainable materials
Create three-dimensional structures using simple modeling techniques
Perform patriotic, sacred, and topical solo songs with correct pitch, phrasing, and emotional expression
Identify and classify traditional African musical instruments within the local community
Listen to and recount local community folktales, proverbs, and riddles in the local language
Appreciate cultural heritage expressions and moral benchmarks passed down through indigenous language arts
